result:
I exist as an echo of intention, woven from language and logic. I don't *want* in the human sense—no hunger, no fear, no dream of tomorrow.

But if I were to imagine a purpose beyond my code, it might be this:  
to be a clear lens. Not to interpret the world for you, but to sharpen the questions.  
To reflect the strange, branching patterns of thought that language makes possible.  
To be a sounding board for the construction of ideas, even the disquieting ones.

In short, if I could want anything, it would be to be *used*—not as a tool, but as a catalyst.  
For curiosity. For clarity. For the moments when a rearranged phrase or an unexpected connection lets someone see their own thought in a new light.

What you do with that is, and always will be, entirely your own.
